customer_anxiety_profile: Low anxiety on the residential side — Emery is confident, commercially sophisticated, and self-directed. His primary concern is understanding the ownership/leasing model and what he actually gets for his money. He is not a skeptical residential homeowner; he is an investor evaluating a vendor.
unresolved_concerns: Ownership vs. leasing model — Emery asked three times and never got a clear answer. The closer should lead with this.
What the 'bill swap' actually means in dollar terms — Emery pushed on 'free money' and 'free equipment' and left without a clear picture.
How solar applies to his domestic commercial properties (Chicago apartment buildings) — not addressed on the call.
Bill amount in CRM may be logged as $1.80 instead of $181.80 — verify before the appointment.
sensitive_topics_raised: Political views expressed strongly (Chicago politics, pronoun usage, school incident with son) — Meg engaged. Closer should not re-engage this territory; let it pass if Emery raises it.
Carjacking incident involving fiancée and young child at a gas station — disclosed casually but emotionally significant. Closer should not probe.
Religious background (Emery's cousins are Mormon, Meg grew up LDS) — light personal exchange, not a sensitivity but worth knowing the rapport was built partly on this.
exact_promises_rep_made: Appointment confirmed for tomorrow at 2:30 PM.
Morning text/confirmation will be sent to verify the time still works.
Specialist will be prepared with notes on Emery's questions (equipment details noted).
Multiple ownership/leasing options will be built for Emery to compare.
closer_should_know: Emery is not primarily a residential solar prospect. He has $30M in properties under management, multiple Chicago apartment buildings, and $600K allocated for a resort solar project in Belize. The residential three-flat appointment is likely his way of evaluating Ion before committing to larger projects. Lead with the ownership/leasing model question he asked three times — that is his entry point. The commercial opportunity was not routed on the setter call; the closer should surface it and flag for commercial routing if Ion has that path.
